comment: Seccomp profile for tempest-sandbox — permissive base with explicit deny of container-escape and kernel-modification syscalls. Keeps ptrace/process_vm_readv/process_vm_writev available for strace, gdb, and ltrace without requiring seccomp:unconfined.

syscalls.comment: Kernel module loading — not needed; blocks rootkit installation
Kernel replace — kexec loads a new kernel, a complete container escape
Filesystem mount operations — containers should not mount filesystems
open_by_handle_at / name_to_handle_at — classic container-escape via file handle to host FS
Reboot syscalls — should never be called inside a sandbox container
Swap management — not needed in a container
Quota control — not needed in a container
BPF — blocks BPF-based privilege escalation paths
Kernel keyring — not needed; can leak credentials between containers
setns / unshare — could be used to escape the current namespace set
Clock modification — containers should use the host clock read-only
